The CVT covers all main disciplines:
The Calvary Ermitage Museum at XQV0Z is mainly open in July-August, Mon-Fri and Sunday 2 pm-6 pm, and during the European Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Calvary Ermitage Museum at XQV0Z is reached on foot via the Chemin du Calvaire from the town (1 h uphill through the 14 chapels), or by car in summer via a forest road.

The Calvary of XQV0Z, presented at the Calvary Ermitage Museum, includes 14 chapels and oratories retracing the stations of the Passion of Christ, built between 1840 and 1878 by Father Ambroise Martin.

The Calvary of XQV0Z, whose history is told at the Ermitage Museum, is the work of Father Ambroise Martin (1791-1863), parish priest of XQV1Z from 1820 until his death, who built 14 chapels between 1840 and 1878.
The Cascade de Bérard via ferrata, at the hamlet of Le Buet in the Vallon de Bérard nature reserve, a playful route of bridges and walkways above the torrent and waterfall.
The Casino of XQV0Z is about 10 minutes by car from Geneva via the D1201, or via the A40, XQV1Z exit. Free parking on site.
The Casino of XQV0Z offers 112 slot machines: 11 video poker, 13 classic reels and 88 video reels from international brands.
The Chablais Museum housed in the XQV0Z charges admission for exhibitions; free for some audiences. Up-to-date prices on thononlesbains.com. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Chablais Museum housed in the XQV0Z offers a permanent exhibition on the history of the territory (lake, fishing, boatbuilding, Savoyard life).
The Chartreux potholes are cavities carved by water erosion in the rock. Several natural basins succeed each other along the waterfall, forming a remarkable geological landscape.

The Chavannes gondola: Online booking is available at pass.lesgets.com, operated directly by SOLEGETS (no reseller). Day passes, single rides and multi-ride cards can be purchased. Tickets are also sold at the lower station ticket office.
The Chavannes gondola: Single ride adult: €7.20, child: €5.40. MTB day pass adult: €34, child: €26. 6-ride card: €27.50. 12-ride card: €49. Free for under 5s. Verified on 9 May 2026.
The Chavannes gondola: Summer peak season (July–August): daily 9:00 – 17:30. Winter: approx. 9:00 – 17:00. Exact dates on lesgets.com. Verified on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Chavannes gondola: Yes, several establishments open in season: Le Chasse Montagne, La DZ, Le Vaffieu, Le Wetzet and the Restaurant du Lac, all with terraces and Alpine views.

The Children's Course at XQV0Z is accessible from 1 metre (approximately age 4-5). The Junior Course requires minimum 1.20 m. The Advanced Adult Course and Hop Up Challenge are accessible from 1.40 m. Acro'Filet, Sensory Forest, Treasure Hunt and Orienteering are accessible at all ages and heights.

The Château de Fonbonne is a heritage building in XQV0Z, a former 14th-century fortified manor. Its garden, laid out in 2011 by the town as a Herbularius (medieval-inspired medicinal garden), is freely accessible and features sage, rosemary, fennel and other aromatic herbs.
The Château de Fonbonne lies in the historic La Touvière district of XQV0Z, in the town centre, near the Thermes. GPS: 46.400° N · 6.593° E.
The Château de Fonbonne stands on the site of a 14th-century fortified manor that defended the new district of La Touvière at XQV0Z.

The Château de XQV0Z was built in 1304 by Hugh of XQV1Z on the Motte de XQV2Z to counter Savoy. Edward of Savoy besieged it for eleven days in 1305 and took it. The castle flourished until the 16th century (92 m long) before being burned down in 1564 and turned into a stone quarry.

The Château de XQV0Z was the seat of a châtellenie of the Counts of Geneva until 1401, when the county was sold to Savoy. Gradually abandoned, it was razed around 1630 by Louis XIII's royal troops during the War of the Mantuan Succession.

The Château du Vuache, of which the 1579 stone is the last witness, was a 14th-century seigneurial stronghold in the County of Geneva. It was abandoned in the mid-17th century when the seat of the seigneury was moved to Faramaz, and its stones were reused.
The Cistercian site of the Abbaye d'Aulps (XQV0Z) is 10 min away by car and extends the heritage outing in the Vallée d'Aulps.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z costs €5 full price, €3 for young people, and is free for under-12s. Free entry on the first Sunday of the month (September-June). Verified 10 June 2026.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is at the Espace Carpano & Pons, 100 place du 11 Novembre, in the centre of XQV1Z (74300). Train station 500 m away.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is free on the first Sunday of every month (September to June), and year-round for under-12s.

The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is open Tue-Sat 2 pm-6 pm from September to June, and every day 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August. Annual closure 11-30 November. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z presents the world's largest collection of horological escapements, the history of the peasant clockmakers of the Arve and the industrial revolution of bar-turning.

The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm in XQV0Z is a traditional farm from 1815, opened to the public in 1994 as an ecomuseum. It presents peasant life in the Haut-Giffre valley, with a collection of 2,000 period utensils.
The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm is on Chemin du Clos Parchet in XQV0Z (74340), on the south-facing slopes above the village, about 10 minutes' walk from the centre. GPS: 46.101° N · 6.701° E.
The Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z displays the tools, sewing machines, manuals and Savoyard footwear of the village cobblers (300 craftsmen in 1880), under the arcades of the Trophée.

The Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z is open mainly from June to August: June and low season Tue-Sat 10 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm; July-August extended hours 10 am-12 pm and 3 pm-7/8 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Cobbler's Museum is at 9 place du Trophée, in the heart of the medieval town of XQV0Z (74540), 20 min from XQV1Z via the A41 (Alby exit).
The Cobbler's Museum of XQV0Z was founded in 1980 and installed in the medieval town in 1999 to preserve the memory of the village's 300 cobblers who made Savoyard footwear in the 19th century.
The Combe des Fours offers an exceptional panorama of XQV0Z, the Aravis and the Fiz massif. The area also has other natural curiosities such as
The Compagnie des Guides de Saint-Gervais offers supervised outings (€50 section 1, €80 section 2, €150 full route) to discover the route safely.

The Counts' Castle of Ternier and La Poype are two fortified structures next to each other on the same hill. The Counts' Castle was a comital residence; La Poype was a fortified manor of the Ternier family. The two were separated by a mound and two ditches.

The Counts' Castle of Ternier dates from the 13th century. It originally belonged to the Ternier family before being ceded to the Counts of Geneva and then to the Counts of Savoy. Damaged by the Bernese and Savoyard wars, it was gradually abandoned.
The Counts' Castle of Ternier is on the hill of Ternier, a hamlet under the commune of XQV0Z (74160), about 2 km south of the town centre, in the Pays de Genève. GPS: 46.139° N · 6.089° E.

The Counts' Castle of Ternier is one of the two medieval remains on the hill of Ternier at XQV0Z. It was the seat of the châtellenie of Ternier, ceded to the Counts of Geneva and then to the Counts of Savoy. Today: wall sections and ditches in open access.
The Counts' Castle of Ternier site is open 24/7, year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the remains and enjoy the panorama. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The Crêt de Châtillon, highest point of XQV0Z, reaches 1699 m according to the Lake XQV1Z Tourist Office. Some sources indicate 1702 m. An orientation table at the summit identifies the surrounding massifs 360°.

The Curalla via ferrata hosts no organised events: it is a free-access mountain route. The Saint-Gervais, Chamonix and XQV0Z guide offices occasionally run collective via ferrata introduction outings, often coupling Curalla in the morning with a second via in the afternoon (Bérard, Saix de Miolène). The XQV1Z Tourist Office publishes a summer mountain activities programme including supervised introductions.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z costs €5 for adults and €3.50 for children. Group rates on request on +33 (0)4 50 72 39 68. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z displays 19th-century hand pumps, historic fire engines, copper helmets, period uniforms and archive videos of major interventions.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z is at the Domaine de Guidou in XQV1Z (74140), 10 min from XQV2Z. Address: 217 route du Moulin de la Glacière.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z is open in May, June and September on weekends 2 pm-6 pm, and every day (public holidays included) 10.30 am-7 pm in July-August. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges has 145 steps to reach the summit and panoramic gallery. The climb takes approximately 10 minutes. Bring suitable shoes and avoid very hot weather.
The Doran valley offers an exceptional photographic setting. Best light in late morning; spray more pronounced in spring during snowmelt.
The Dronières Lake loop is 2 km long, about 30 minutes on foot. The path is stabilised, accessible to strollers, and starts from the tennis car park.
The Dronières Lake loop is accessible to strollers thanks to its stabilised path. The adjacent aquatic centre is equipped for people with reduced mobility.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z features 7 playful wooden modules that make the walker's ball roll, descend and run — wooden flippers, ramps, bends.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z is designed for families with children from age 3-4: easy trail, gradual descent, child-friendly modules.

The Débaroule at XQV0Z is included in the summer Pass Découverte or accessible with a pedestrian pass for the Patinoire cable car. 2026 prices to be confirmed at forfait.laclusaz.com. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z runs during the summer season (mid-June/early July to end of August), depending on the summer lift calendar. Check forfait.laclusaz.com. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z takes 30 to 45 minutes to descend, depending on your pace and the time spent at each module. Longer if continued with the Luge des Bois at the Bossonnet.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z unfolds at altitude, through the pastures and forests of the Aravis massif, with views over the range and the village.
The Débaroule descends from the arrival of the Patinoire cable car (XQV0Z) towards the Bossonnet area, along an easy, shaded trail.
The Débaroule is a playful high-altitude trail in XQV0Z: from the top of the Patinoire cable car, walkers descend on foot to the Bossonnet, accompanying their wooden ball through 7 playful modules.
The Débaroule is an original outdoor summer activity in XQV0Z: a nature-based playful trail that makes a change from the classic high-altitude treetop courses.
The Débaroule is part of XQV0Z's summer Pass Découverte, which combines several activities on the domain (pedestrian lifts, attractions).
The Dévalkart is in the heart of the XQV0Z / La Croix-Fry resort, in the Aravis massif (74230). Car access from XQV1Z via XQV2Z (~40 min) or XQV3Z (~15 min).
The Dévalkart is mainly a summer activity (June-September). In winter, the resort is open for alpine skiing and traditional snow sledging takes over.

The Dévalkart is mainly open from June to September (subject to weather and resort opening). Booking is generally not required — tickets on site. Expect €5-8 per descent, with 5/10/day passes at lower per-descent rates. The site is at the XQV0Z / La Croix-Fry resort, accessible by car from XQV1Z via XQV2Z (~40 min) or from XQV3Z (~15 min). Resort parking, chairlift departure close to the Dévalkart. Combines well with a family day at the resort and a mountain restaurant lunch.
The Dévalkart is mainly open from June to September, depending on weather and resort opening. Check daily opening at manigod.com or the Tourist Office.
The Erraticaborne is a panoramic and artistic shelter installed on the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail: an original stop combining refuge, viewpoint and artwork, to discover along the way.
The Eterlou Trail is open every day from 1 May to 31 October, weather permitting. In winter, the site is used for tobogganing and snow play.
The Eterlou Trail takes about 50 minutes, for a loop of about 600 m with no difficulty.
The Eterlou Walking Trail is at Pré-la-Joux, right next to the chairlift, in the high-altitude sector of XQV0Z.

The Fairy Trail at XQV0Z is accessible from age 3 (under parental supervision). The main course is accessible from 1.20 m height (adult supervision mandatory for children under 12). Canyoning is accessible from age 8 with an adult.
The Faune museum and the Maison de la Belle Vallée are next door. The village is also a starting point for hiking and mountain biking in the Brevon valley.
The Faune museum and the Maison de la Belle Vallée are right next door — visit can be extended over a half-day.
The Ferme de Chosal Trail features donkeys, sheep, goats, billy goats, pigs, hens, pheasants, guinea fowl, rabbits and guinea pigs in their respective pens.
The Ferme de Chosal Trail is open from 5 April to 3 November, 10 am to 6 pm, with last entry at 4.30 pm (information verified on the official website on 10 June 2026, subject to seasonal changes).
The Ferme de Chosal is at 98 route de l'usine Chosal, 74350 XQV0Z, in XQV1Z - 10 min from XQV2Z via the D 1201.
The Follow the Fly trail starts from Place du Trophée, in the heart of the village of XQV0Z. It forms a 2 km loop with 12 stops through the lanes and along the Chéran.

The Follow the Fly trail takes its name from the nail the cobblers of XQV0Z fixed under shoe soles, whose head looked like fly wings, and from the many fly-fishers on the Chéran. Flies drawn on the ground mark out the route.
The Gourmet
The Grand Balcon Sud links Planpraz to Flégère along the Aiguilles Rouges crest at 2,000–2,500 m — one of the great Alpine traverses, accessed from Planpraz.

The Grand Montoir passage on XQV0Z is airy and steep but secured by cables, chains and metal handrails. It can alarm those sensitive to heights. If unsure, ascend and descend via the Petit Montoir (longer but without exposed sections). Avoid the Grand Montoir in wet weather, snow or ice.
The Grand Tour
The Grands Montets is a benchmark freeride destination: Combe de la Pendant, Glacier des Rognons, Combe Grisette. Guide accompaniment is mandatory for glacier itineraries.

The Grands Montets is a winter ski destination (December–early May). It is completely closed throughout summer 2026 due to the new 3S cable car construction. Full reopening — including access to the 3,275 m summit — is planned for December 2026 according to Compagnie du XQV0Z. For summer 2026, visitors wanting a view over the Argentière glacier should consider the XQV1Z or Montenvers instead.

The Grands Montets is included in the Chamonix Le Pass: adult day pass 59.20–74 € depending on the period. An AlpiSki AGM ski touring ticket is also available at 36.80 € for adults (desk purchase only). Verified 28 April 2026.
The Grands Montets offers one of the only direct views of the Drus (3,754 m) and Aiguille Verte (4,122 m) from the slopes in XQV0Z, from the Lognan sector.
The Granges de Servette Museum is at the hamlet of Collongette, 224 Chemin des Granges de Servette, 74140 XQV0Z, 25 min from Thonon and 20 min from Geneva.
The Granges de Servette Museum is open from 29 June to 31 August, every day 3 pm to 7 pm. Occasional openings during the Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Granges de Servette Museum presents a permanent contemporary-art collection (the Lyon school since 1958) and more than 1,000 antique tools of traditional crafts.
The Hameau du Père Noël, open since 1989 in neighbouring Saint-Blaise, is one of the oldest permanent Christmas attractions in Europe. A
The Herbularius garden at the Château de Fonbonne in XQV0Z is freely accessible, especially during the growing season (spring to autumn). Check ville-evian.fr. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Herbularius garden at the Château de Fonbonne is laid out in raised beds enclosed by chestnut wattle, with a central water feature and a bower — faithful medieval aesthetics.
The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne combines well with a visit to the Évian Thermes, the Palais Lumière or the Jardins de l'Eau du Pré Curieux.

The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne mainly features medieval-inspired aromatic and medicinal plants: sage, rosemary, fennel, horseradish, lemon balm, arranged in raised beds around a water feature.
The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne offers a carefully composed photo setting: chestnut wattle, water feature, bower flowering with climbing roses.
The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne offers a free stroll among medieval-inspired medicinal and aromatic plants, in free access.

The Jaillet Gondola opens summer 2026: 4 July–30 August. Exact hours to confirm on lesportesdumontblanc.fr — 2025 reference: 9:15 am–5:45 pm. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to change.
The Jaillet Gondola: Based on 2025 prices: adult return €19, child 5–14 €14.50, adult single €11.50, day pass €22.50. Under 5 free. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026.
The LPO regularly organizes maintenance work parties (mowing orchid dunes, removing invasive plants, maintaining edges and ponds) open to volunteers. Contact: haute-savoie[a]lpo.fr.
The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail is practicable from May to October depending on snow cover, with free access year-round once the road is cleared. In winter, the area is used for snowshoeing.
The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail starts at the lake car park, above the village of XQV0Z, in the Vallée d'XQV1Z, at about 1,170 m altitude.

The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail tells 20,000 years of geological history of the Vallée d'XQV0Z: great glaciers, slow retreat, peat bog formation, and the story of an intriguing erratic boulder left on site.

The Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum at XQV1Z is an immersive museum devoted to 19th-century Savoyard life on the lakeshore. Indoor trails with puzzle-games and an outdoor puzzle trail, for families and school groups.
The Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum is on Route de l'Église in XQV1Z (74320), about 8 km south of XQV2Z on the western shore of the lake. GPS: 45.864° N · 6.139° E. Telephone: +33 (0)4 50 52 41 05.
The Little Feathers House (13:30): activities about this season's baby raptors. The Rabbit-odrome (17:00): dedicated space for young children with direct contact with rabbits. Forest playground.
The Mechanical Music Museum is on Place de la Mairie, at the heart of the XQV0Z resort (74260), at 1,172 m altitude. 25 min from XQV1Z via D902/D328.
The Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z displays 1,000 instruments: chimes, clocks, music boxes, orchestrions, mechanical pianos, automata, phonographs and a reconstructed Belle Époque funfair.

The Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z is open from 21 December 2025 to 31 October 2026, every day 2 pm-7 pm (except Saturdays from 1 January to 30 April). Annual closure 1 November to 20 December. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Mont Veyrier cable car was inaugurated on 29 July 1934 by banker Léon Laydernier. It transported 2 million passengers over 50 years, reaching 70,000 passengers in 1948. It held two world records: 1,525 m cable span and 808 m elevation gain without intermediate pylons. Closed in 1984 for economic reasons (cost of safety upgrades), the upper station was demolished in 2001 after a fatal accident in 1998.
The Mont-Chéry gondola: A 360° panorama: Mont Blanc massif, the Aravis range, Lac XQV0Z, the Swiss Alps and the Dents du Midi. One of the widest accessible mountain views in the area.
The Mont-Chéry gondola: Online at pass.lesgets.com, operated directly by SOLEGETS (no reseller). Pedestrian, MTB and multi-ride cards available. Also sold at the lower station ticket office.

The Mont-Chéry gondola: Single ride adult: €7.20, child: €5.40. Summit round-trip adult: €10, child: €7. Family pass (min. 4 people): adult €9, child €6.30. 6-ride card: €27.50. Free under 5. Verified 28 April 2026.
The Mont-Chéry gondola: Summer peak season (July–Aug): daily 9:00 – 17:30. Winter: approx. 9:00 – 16:45. Exact dates on lesgets.com. Verified 28 April 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Morclan Trail is open from 15 June to 15 September every day, subject to favourable weather. Off-season, it is not maintained and may be covered in snow.
The Morclan Trail is rated 'very easy' by the XQV0Z Tourist Office, but it is a steady climb in alpine pastures: 3.5 km from the Tenne bend up to Super-XQV1Z, about 2 h 30 of walking.
The Morclan Trail offers magnificent landscapes with views of the 'arc' of the Vallée d'XQV0Z and the Mont de Grange, and features educational panels on wildlife, flora and the mountain environment.
The Morclan Trail reaches Super-XQV0Z, starting point for other walks and a high-altitude area also accessible by cable car.
The Morclan Trail reaches Super-XQV0Z: depending on the summer opening calendar of the Super-XQV1Z cable car, you can go down by lift rather than on foot. Check the dates on chatel.com.
The Morclan Trail starts at the Tenne bend, at the end of the route du Petit-XQV0Z in XQV1Z (about 2 km from the village centre).
The Museum of Prehistory and Geology of XQV0Z
The Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z displays more than 20,000 objects of XQV1Z peasant life gathered by Bernard Lacroix: forge, bakery, cobbler's workshop, old-time grocery and tools.
The Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z is located in the village centre of XQV1Z (74890), 20 min from XQV2Z and 25 min from XQV3Z, in an 18th-century farmhouse.
The Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z is open mainly on Thursdays in July-August for guided tours, 3 pm-7 pm. Openings by appointment for groups. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Nature Detective booklet for the XQV0Z walking loop can be downloaded free of charge from the Syndicat du Vuache website (pays-du-vuache.fr), or picked up at the Monts du Genevois Tourist Office and at the town hall.
The Nature Detective loop on the Vuache Trail in XQV0Z takes about 1 hr 30 to 2 hr 30 depending on the children's pace and the number of stops to answer the quiz and observe nature.
The Nature Detective trail in XQV0Z is open 24/7 year-round (except frost/snow). Daytime is preferable to read the panels. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Nature Detective walking loop in XQV0Z is entirely free: open-access trail, booklet downloadable for free from pays-du-vuache.fr. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The Nature Detective walking loop is a free family route on the Vuache Nature Trail in XQV0Z, based on Nature Detective game booklets from the Syndicat du Vuache: quizzes, crosswords, observation, with answers hidden on 15 educational panels.
The Nature Museum at XQV0Z is made up of 4 spaces: naturalised mountain wildlife, a glass-fronted hive with 30,000 bees, a teaching garden of medicinal plants, and a conservation orchard.
The Nature Museum at XQV0Z is open to the general public during school holidays, and year-round by reservation for groups and schools. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Nature Museum is at 335 Route de Beauregard, locality Les Choseaux, 74540 XQV0Z, south of Lake XQV1Z. 25 min from XQV2Z via A41 Alby exit.
The Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is FREE for everyone every Wednesday, and all year for under-22s, students and several reduced-rate categories.
The Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is at 5 place de la Manufacture, in the former tobacco manufactory (74150). About 25 min from XQV1Z via the A41.

The Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is open Wednesday to Saturday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm. Closed Sunday-Tuesday and public holidays. Annual closures: 8-26 August and 23-26 December 2026. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z presents the history of the town and the Albanais: paintings, prints, sculptures, crafts, industry (tobacco manufactory, Tefal). 2026 exhibition 'XQV1Z, town or countryside?'.
The Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z features a 1783 Savoyard farmhouse, a collection of skis and winter gear from 1900 (sealskins, sleds, snowshoes) and alpine peasant life.
The Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is open year-round, every day on request. SMS booking required on +33 (0)7 62 21 88 68. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Old Ski Museum sits in the village centre of XQV0Z (74360), a Portes du Soleil resort in the XQV1Z Valley. About 40 min from Thonon.
The PAYSALP Peasant Museum at XQV0Z is open Monday to Friday 10 am-12.30 pm and 1.30 pm-5 pm (until 6 pm from 10 July to 1 September). Verified 10 June 2026.
The PAYSALP Peasant Museum at XQV0Z offers a 500 m² immersive exhibition on mountain life 1850-1950, with a 1924 classroom where you can sit a certificat d'études.
The PAYSALP Peasant Museum is at 628 avenue de Savoie, 74250 XQV0Z, 25 min from XQV1Z (D907) and 25 min from XQV2Z (D26). Free on-site parking.
The PAYSALP Peasant Museum's Absurd Game is a life-size board game played inside the museum (€10 adult, €7 child, by reservation). A fun activity for families and groups.
The Palais served as a
The Parc de l'Imperial is a public garden open year-round, accessible 24/7, with no fence or closing times. Municipal services and the Brasserie du Parc keep their own hours.

The Parc de l'Imperial is on avenue d'Albigny, a 10-minute walk from the Pâquier. By public transport: Sibra bus, Impérial stop. By car: paid underground car parks Hôtel de Ville and Impérial nearby. By bike, the Lake XQV0Z greenway runs along the foot of the park.

The Parc de l'Imperial is pleasant all year, but the rose garden and beds are in full bloom from May to September. Summer pairs the park with the Impérial beach and lake cruises, while autumn and winter offer a quieter walk.

The Parc des Dronières surrounding the Dronières Lake offers an animal park with fallow deer, red deer and mouflons (feeding not allowed), the TactiQ tree-top adventure course, a 2.5 km fitness trail, a permanent orienteering course, and new playgrounds delivered in 2026.

The Parmelan plateau is a spectacular karstic anticline composed of urgonian limestone forming a vast lapiaz riddled with faults, sinkholes and caves. The ascent to XQV0Z takes either the Grand Montoir (an airy passage equipped with cables and chains, shorter but steep) or the Petit Montoir (longer but less steep). The plateau offers a 360° panoramic view over the Genevois, Lake XQV1Z, the Bauges, the Aravis and the XQV2Z massif.

The Pass'Beun is a day package at €18 giving unlimited access to all XQV0Z activities: swimming, canoe, pedalo, stand-up paddle, Télébeune (aquatic zip-line), Wibit (inflatable structures), trampolines, sports fields. Indiana Parc adventure course is not included.
The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum is free for children under 12. The adult price is to be confirmed with the XQV1Z Town Hall on +33 (0)4 50 02 97 76. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum is in central XQV1Z (74230), 30 min from XQV2Z via the D909 and 15 min from XQV3Z.

The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum is open Monday to Saturday with varying hours: Monday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Tuesday 9 am-12 pm, Wednesday 9 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm, Thu-Fri 9 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Saturday 9.30 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-6 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum presents geology, prehistory, crafts, Baroque religious art, fauna and flora of the Aravis, and the firefighters' costume of the famous Fête des Sapeurs.
The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum was founded in 1937 by François Cochat, who donated his rich collection. His son Lucien Cochat continued his work. The museum bears their names.
The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z displays every Buisse-Bottazzi film-projector model (1946-2007), complemented by period posters tracing the history of popular cinema.
The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z is open every Tuesday in July and August, 2.30 pm-5.30 pm. Outside the summer season, opening by appointment on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum is at 43 bis rue du Bourg Neuf, in the centre of XQV0Z, 20 min from Geneva and 15 min from XQV1Z.
The Pitchoun trail welcomes children from age 3, then progressive trails (yellow, green, blue) by height. High sporty trails (red, black) are accessible from around 10-12 years or 1.40 m.
The Plage d'Amphion holds the
The Plaine-Joux paragliding takeoff area is accessible by car via D43 from XQV0Z (8 km) or XQV1Z (10 km). Free parking on site. Seasonal Y85 bus and bike/mountain bike access possible.
The Plaine-Joux paragliding takeoff area is located at 1,360 meters altitude, facing XQV0Z, with 760 meters elevation drop to landings at Chedde or Marlioz.
The Plateau des Moises (XQV0Z) and its 'Secrets de fées' trails are a 10-minute drive away  perfect to extend the family outing.
The Pléney Gondola is the main access to XQV0Z's world-class MTB descent bikepark, with trails for all levels. MTB-specific ticket required.

The Pléney Gondola runs 5 July–31 August 2026 daily 9:00 am–5:30 pm. Evening openings to 7:00 pm Tues & Thurs 8 July–28 August. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

The Pointe Percée Geological Trail is generally walked from June to October, after the snowmelt and before the first snowfalls. Mountain weather remains very changeable: check forecasts and snow cover before setting off.
The Pointe Percée Geological Trail starts at the Col des Annes (1721 m), in the commune of XQV0Z, and climbs up to the Pointe Percée refuge at around 2164 m, at the foot of the eponymous summit.
The Pré Curieux Water Gardens
The Pré Curieux Water Gardens in Évian: outstanding water garden on the shore of Lake Geneva, accessed by shuttle boat. Guided tour, April-October.
The Quick Jump at XQV0Z is a controlled free-fall experience from over 10 m — a strong thrill, extra to the tree-top ticket.
The Rovorée manor and La Châtaignière host temporary exhibitions, artist residencies and cultural activities (variable programming).
The Ruins of the castle of XQV0Z are in the centre of the village of XQV1Z (74420), in the Vallée Verte, about 25 km south-east of XQV2Z.
The Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z (at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce) mainly offers guided tours on Friday at 3 pm during school holidays. Outside holidays, opening by appointment. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z exhibits around fifty objects (out of 600+ kept): 17th-18th-century Baroque polychrome sculptures, liturgical gold and silverware, paintings and embroidered priestly vestments.
The Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z is in the former 18th-century presbytery of the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce hamlet (approx. 1,170 m), 8 km from central Saint-Gervais, next to the Baroque church.
The Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z is reached from central Saint-Gervais via the D43 (8 km, road cleared of snow year-round). Parking on the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce hamlet square.
The Secret of the Allobroges
The Secrets de fées booklet is sold for €5 either at the Alpes du XQV0Z Tourist Office (XQV1Z / Boëge) or directly at the Foyer nordique des Moises, at the start of the trails.

The Secrets de fées discovery trail has four themed routes at the Plateau des Moises: Les Sources Claires (Hydris, 1 h 30), Les Prés Fleuris (Florine, 1 h), Le Beau Pays (Bohême, 1 h 15) and Les Vertes Forêts (Sylvaine, 1 h 15).
The Secrets de fées trails have free access year-round. Only the discovery booklet with its accessories is paid: €5 at the Alpes du XQV0Z Tourist Office or at the Foyer des Moises.

The Sensory Forest at XQV0Z is fully accessible to pushchairs and wheelchairs (except the balance workshop and barefoot trail). Children's and Junior courses may suit some prosthesis wearers (contact the park beforehand). A free companion is offered for any person with disabilities unable to complete the activity independently.

The Sensory Forest is accessible to pushchairs and wheelchairs (except balance workshop and barefoot trail). Children's and Junior courses may suit some prosthesis wearers (contact park first). Free companion for any person with disabilities unable to complete the activity independently.
The Super-XQV0Z Gondola runs 26 June–31 August 2026 daily. Exact times to confirm (2025: 9:00 am–5:15 pm). Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The TMB is traditionally walked

The Tactiq Game is an outdoor adventure game in the style of an "escape forest" at the heart of the Dronières forest, blending riddles and observation. It costs €10 per person, in addition to or independently of the tree-top courses.

The Thiou floodgates are a hydraulic system of 62 floodgates spread over 6 km, installed in 1874 by engineer Sadi Carnot, regulating Lake XQV0Z's level and once powering the riverside forges, paper mills and tanneries.
The Thiou is

The Thiou is a small river 3.5 km long, the natural outflow of Lake XQV0Z. It crosses the old town and joins the Fier - a tributary of the Rhône - at Meythet. With an average flow of 8 m³/s, it is the historic and scenic axis of XQV1Z.
The Thiou quays host the Tuesday, Friday and Sunday markets, along with terraces, ice-cream parlours and craft shops.

The Thiou, natural outflow of Lake XQV0Z, runs for 3.5 km through the old town to the Fier. Pont des Amours, canal du Vassé, Palais de l'Île, Sadi Carnot floodgates: XQV1Z's heritage backbone is best discovered on foot.
The Tour of Lac du Môle is a loop of about 1.6 km for 21 m of positive elevation, with a high point at 613 m altitude. It's a very short walk, perfect for a half-hour stroll.
The Tour of Lac du Môle is open 24/7, year-round. Spring to autumn is preferable for bird-watching and walking comfort. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Tour of Lac du Môle walk is entirely free: an open-access trail, no ticketing. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Tour of Lac du Môle walk is in XQV0Z (74250), at the entrance to the village of XQV1Z, at the foot of the Château de XQV2Z, in the XQV3Z region. GPS: 46.140° N · 6.420° E.
The Tour of XQV0Z Village is a very easy family walk of about 1 h 30 starting from the centre of the village.
The Tour of XQV0Z Village map is available free at the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'XQV1Z tourist information office in XQV2Z itself.

The Tour of XQV0Z Village takes in the village centre, the Abbaye d'XQV1Z (12th century), the Maison du Fromage XQV2Z, the banks of the Dranse and a viewpoint over the bell tower and the peaks of the Vallée d'XQV3Z.
The Villa du Pré Curieux, an elegant Norman-style residence, frames the garden. Its history and architecture are described during the tour.
The Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z is open year-round on Thursdays and Saturdays, 2 pm-6 pm. Guided tours on Saturdays at 10.30 am and 2.30 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Vintage Aircraft Museum is at XQV0Z, on the XQV1Z-XQV2Z aerodrome site, on the northern shore of Lake Geneva, 10 min from XQV3Z.

The Wellness area spans 380 m² with eight naturally lit treatment cabins (including one double cabin), a whole-body cryotherapy cabin, two single-sex hammams, a mixed sauna, and rest areas with views of Lake XQV0Z. Services range from personalized relaxation massages to signature treatments with evian® water (hydromassage bath, rain shower massage, natural mineral water jet pool). The Fitness area offers group classes in the gym and thermal pool (aquabike, aquarun, aquapilates), a cardio-strength room with lake views, as well as memberships and pay-per-visit options.
The Wibit – inflatable aquatic obstacle course
The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z costs €4 adult, €3 child 4-16, €3.20 student, free under 4. Combined two-museum ticket: €7 adult / €5 child. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z displays more than 200 naturalised animals of the XQV1Z mountains: chamois, ibex, marmots, lynx, black grouse, golden eagles, bearded vultures, with sound terminals.

The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z opens mainly during school holidays: April Monday-Wednesday-Friday, and July-August Monday to Friday 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-4 pm (Thursday 2 pm-6 pm). Verified 10 June 2026.
The Wildlife Museum is beneath the XQV0Z town hall, on the village square (74470), at the heart of the Brevon valley in Haut-Chablais. 30 min from Thonon.
The XQV0Z
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail can be looped by returning to the village along the Saint-Jacques-de-Compostelle path, a.k.a. the GR Balcon du XQV1Z GR65 (20 min).
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail features the Erraticaborne, a panoramic and artistic shelter installed on the route — an original and photogenic stop.

The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail is a shaded trail in the Pays de Genève where each walker can create an ephemeral land-art work (stone, wood, soil) and discover wooden sculptures installed by artists. Free access, ideal for families.
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail is entirely free: the trail is in free access, and land-art creations using the natural materials on site are unlimited. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail is freely accessible 24/7, year-round. Spring to autumn is best for the light and for comfortable creating. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail starts from the village of XQV1Z (74350), with local signs. GPS: 46.070° N · 6.070° E. Free parking in the village.
The XQV0Z Art Nature Trail stops at the Bougerie pond, where 3 picnic tables await walkers — an ideal family break.
The XQV0Z Chalet-Refuge (2,002 m) offers traditional Savoyard cuisine and overnight accommodation by reservation (June to September). Panoramic terraces facing XQV1Z and Charvin.
The XQV0Z Ice Rink
The XQV0Z Tennis & Padel Club
The XQV0Z also houses the XQV1Z Tourist Office, a starting point for exploring the town, the lake, the Château de Ripaille and the Chablais.

The XQV0Z are open daily from 15 March to 15 October, from 9:30 am to 6:15 pm in low season and until 7:15 pm in summer (15 June – 10 September). The ticket office closes 1 hour before the site. The site is closed from 16 October to 14 March.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z cannot be visited inside. The external view from the road is free. No ticketing. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z is an excellent example of 19th-century "folly" architecture, deliberately imitating a medieval castle — the work of the architect Bruger.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z is best discovered as part of a heritage stop in the Arve valley, between XQV2Z and XQV3Z.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z is discovered from the road and the grounds: a pseudo-medieval 19th-century silhouette with towers, battlements and decorative machicolations.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z is discovered from the road: the discreet silhouette of a 16th-century fortified manor, partly screened by vegetation.
The XQV0Z at XQV1Z is visible from the road 24/7, year-round. No visiting hours (interior closed). Verified on 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z biotope pond is a large ecological water feature planted with aquatic vegetation, created during the 2010-2013 renovation. It encourages biodiversity (dragonflies, wetland plants, insects) and forms the park's landscape heart.
The XQV0Z circuit is a 2 km loop accessible to all, achievable in 30 minutes' walking at moderate pace. The trail is stroller-accessible and features children's play areas throughout the route.
The XQV0Z combines easily with a visit to the XQV1Z Museum of Watchmaking and Bar-Turning, 5 km away — industrial heritage of the XQV2Z.
The XQV0Z combines with a "Bas-Chablais châteaux" day: Avully (XQV1Z), Buffavent (XQV2Z), Ripaille (Thonon).
The XQV0Z combines with a visit to the Château de Ripaille (2 km away), the second great heritage site of XQV1Z.
The XQV0Z covers 4.1 hectares with a maximum depth of 8.7 metres, at 1,452 m elevation. Its catchment area is 90.6 hectares.

The XQV0Z flows year-round, but the experience varies greatly by season. The flow is spectacular in spring (May–June) during snowmelt, with flow reaching several cubic metres per second and impressive water sprays — surroundings become slippery and dangerous. In summer (July–October), flow decreases but remains visible, direct car access and the site is very busy on weekends and in peak season. In autumn (September–October), visitor numbers drop, light is beautiful and surrounding forest displays colours. In winter (December–April), the road is closed after Salvagny; access is on foot or by snowshoe (1h round-trip), offering spectacular winter scenery with partially frozen waterfall and assured tranquility. Best light for photography: morning (9 am–11 am) or late afternoon, avoid noon (backlighting).

The XQV0Z formed on the night of 11–12 March 1943, during a landslide that blocked the course of the Brévon river. About ten chalets were swept away, and the submerged hamlet of L'Écondu now lies beneath the water.

The XQV0Z has outdoor showers near the first aid post, volleyball and pétanque courts, table tennis tables, a fitness trail with 23 apparatus, equipped picnic areas, public toilets, free parking and dog waste bins. A bird observatory is located at the northern end of lake 3.

The XQV0Z hike is challenging and reserved for experienced hikers. Elevation gain of 850 to 1,200 m depending on the route. The slope is sustained after Pavillon du XQV1Z, with steep and rocky passages on the summit section (Épaule or Panse). Bring proper hiking boots, water and warm clothing.
The XQV0Z hosts XQV1Z's FestiGrat's music festival every summer: free concerts in the exceptional setting of the estate.
The XQV0Z in XQV1Z is open continuously, 24/7, every day all year. The park is unfenced and does not close. Verified on 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z in XQV1Z occupies the former 18th-century domain built by Joachim de la Grange, Count of XQV2Z, then the Clos du Chalet (1857) built by Laurent-Marie Humbert, a watchmaker who made his fortune in Paris.

The XQV0Z in XQV1Z sits on the site of the former châteaux of Count Joachim de la Grange (18th c.) and the Clos du Chalet (1857), demolished or transformed. The park keeps their memory and their views.
The XQV0Z is 10 min on foot from XQV1Z station. TAC bus lines 3 and 4 serve the Jean Jaurès stop. From Geneva, allow 5-10 min by public transport: the park is 100 m from the Franco-Swiss border.

The XQV0Z is a 15th-century fortified manor built by the lords of Langin as a fortified hunting residence. Attacked and taken by the Genevans in 1590 (cannonball marks visible), it was listed as a Historic Monument in 1944.
The XQV0Z is a 17th-century château (1666) built by the Gerbaix de Sonnaz family on the ruins of the medieval fortress of Thonon. It now houses the Chablais Museum and the XQV1Z Tourist Office.

The XQV0Z is a fortified manor mainly built in the 16th century by the lords of La Croix, later linked to the Rochette family. Its history is tied to that of the XQV1Z. Inscribed MH 1988, listed MH 1994.
The XQV0Z is a pseudo-medieval 19th-century construction (1863) deliberately imitating a medieval castle — the work of the architect Bruger for Ernest de Boigne, in the Romantic taste of the time.
The XQV0Z is accessible on foot from central Chamonix (45 min walk from Grépon car park via the Chemin à Batioret) or in 10 minutes from the car park on the XQV1Z tunnel access road.

The XQV0Z is accessible year-round, but optimal conditions fall between April and November. In spring (April–June), flow is at its maximum thanks to snowmelt and the Pélerins Glacier — the spectacle is particularly impressive, with tumultuous water and powerful roaring. In summer (July–August), the waterfall offers a much-appreciated cool refuge on hot days, and the adjoining chalet-café is open for refreshment on the terrace. In autumn (September–October), the colours of the surrounding forests enhance the setting, and visitor numbers drop. In winter, access depends on snow conditions — the trail may be covered with ice in places and become difficult to traverse; the waterfall can even be partially frozen during cold snaps, forming ephemeral natural sculptures. For an optimal experience and favourable light for photography, choose a morning visit.
The XQV0Z is at 1,486 m between XQV1Z (XQV2Z, 74) and La Giettaz (Savoie, 73), on the D909. It marks the boundary between the two departments.
The XQV0Z is closely tied to the seigneurial history of the XQV1Z — a teaching stop on the system of fortified manors in the Arve valley.

The XQV0Z is open April to mid-November. April, May, June, September, October: Friday, Saturday, Sunday and public holidays 1 pm to 6 pm (last entry 5:30 pm). July–August: daily 10 am to 7 pm (last entry 6:30 pm). Exceptional openings during Easter and All Saints' holidays: daily 1 pm–6 pm. Verified on the official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

The XQV0Z is open Tue-Sat 9.30 am-12 pm and 2 pm-5.30 pm out of season, and daily 9.30 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August (except Sunday morning). Annual closure 1 November-15 December. Verified 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z is open daily 10 April to 7 September 2026 from 10:00 AM to 6:30 PM (ticket office closes at 5:30 PM). 8 September to 4 October 2026: 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM (ticket office closes at 4:30 PM). Annual closure early October through mid-April. Verified on official website 6 June 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

The XQV0Z is open year-round, but the summer season (May to September) offers the most activities: supervised swimming in July and August, café-restaurant open mid-April to mid-September, ideal conditions for windsurfing and paddling. Spring and autumn are quieter, perfect for fishing and walking. In winter, the site remains accessible for lakeside walks, but water sports are suspended. Note: refurbishment works from September 2025 to June 2026 will limit access to swimming and fishing lakes.
The XQV0Z is reachable on foot from central XQV1Z. XQV2Z Express SNCF station 10 minutes away. Public car parks in the centre. CGN landing stage very close.

The XQV0Z is still inhabited by the Menthon family, owners since the 11th century (23 generations). Count Olivier de Menthon is the current guardian. It is a private residence open to the public for guided tours.
The XQV0Z karting venue
The XQV0Z lies in the commune of XQV1Z (74950), in the Arve valley (XQV2Z), near XQV3Z. It is visible from the main road. GPS: 46.059° N · 6.552° E.
The XQV0Z lies on the Boissy hillside at XQV1Z (74140), at 733 m altitude, in the Bas-Chablais. GPS: 46.303° N · 6.325° E. About 15 km from XQV2Z.
The XQV0Z offers a classic photo setting: 17th-century façade, view over Lake Geneva and the roofs of Thonon — a subject popular with heritage enthusiasts.
The XQV0Z offers a heritage photography subject from the roadside: a discreet fortified manor in its leafy setting in the Arve valley.
The XQV0Z offers a spectacular photographic subject: a neo-Gothic 19th-century silhouette atop the Boissy hillside, with Lake Geneva in the background.

The XQV0Z opens every day on a seasonal schedule: 7 am-10 pm in June, July and August; 7 am-8 pm in April, May, September and October; 7 am-7 pm from November to March (source: XQV1Z Tourisme listing, verified on 10 June 2026).
The XQV0Z reaches 1,486 m, which makes it the lowest road pass crossing the Aravis range.
The XQV0Z section threads through the

The XQV0Z site is still reachable on foot, but since 6 January 2026 a municipal order has closed the Bété trail. Only the climb from the Morsulaz car park is usable. Check the commune website before setting out.
The XQV0Z sits on the heights of XQV1Z (74440), at the quai du Bras de Fer, overlooking the village centre. About 15 min from XQV2Z via the D902.
The XQV0Z stands about 800 m north-west of the village of XQV1Z (74890), in the Bas-Chablais, 15 km from XQV2Z. GPS: 46.288° N · 6.406° E.
The XQV0Z stands on place du Château, in the heart of XQV1Z, in the upper town facing Lake Geneva, near the town hall. GPS: 46.373° N · 6.478° E.
The XQV0Z was built by the lords of La Croix, later linked by marriage to the Rochette family — noble lines of the XQV1Z from the 16th to 18th centuries.
The XQV0Z was built in 1666 by the Gerbaix de Sonnaz family, one of the oldest families in Savoy, on the ruins of the medieval fortress of Thonon built by Marie of Burgundy, wife of Amadeus VIII.
The XQV0Z was built in 1666 by the Gerbaix de Sonnaz, one of the oldest families in Savoy — a key step in Thonon's seigneurial heritage.

The XQV0Z was built in 1863 by the architect Bruger at the request of General Count Ernest de Boigne, on the ruins of a medieval fortress. Sold in 1893 to a Lyon banker, later acquired by the SIVOM of Bas-Chablais.
The XQV0Z was built in 1863 under Ernest de Boigne, heir to the family fortune made in India by General Benoît de Boigne — Savoyard heritage.

The XQV0Z water sports base is a few minutes' walk from Évian town centre, from Évian SNCF station (XQV1Z Express to Geneva via XQV2Z) and from the quay car parks. CGN boats dock at the neighbouring landing stage.
The XQV0Z water sports base is on avenue du Général Dupas, on Évian beach by Lake Geneva, 10 minutes' walk from Évian SNCF station. GPS: 46.400° N · 6.583° E.

The XQV0Z water sports base is open in the summer season daily from 1 pm to 7 pm, with a late opening until 9 pm on Thursdays. Lifeguarded beach June-September 12 pm-6 pm (11 am-7 pm in July-August). Checked on 10 June 2026.

The XQV0Z water sports base offers paddle, kayak (single or double), sailing, rowing, diving, freediving, pedalos and rowing boats. Activities are self-guided or supervised by the Évian Sailing Club, on Lake Geneva.
The XQV0Z, perched on the upper town of XQV1Z, offers an open view over Lake Geneva, the Swiss shore (Lausanne, Vevey) and the Jura range.
The abbey stands at the heart of the village, ranked among the Most Beautiful Villages of France. Fountains, shrines and mountain chalets testify to alpine heritage.
The activity is

The activity is aimed at children from 4-6 years (dedicated zone), teens, families and groups (birthdays, stag/hen, seminars, schools outside school hours). Wide choice of packages: single session, multi-session passes, birthday deals with private room for cake and drinks. The ultimate bad-weather indoor activity in the valley, complementing the Grand Massif and XQV0Z resorts. Just-Jump non-slip socks are mandatory (sold on site, reusable).

The activity is exclusively winter and depends on snow (typically December to March). Ride prices ~€50-80/person, kennel visits ~€10-15/person, longer outings on quote. Booking essential, several weeks ahead in high season (Christmas, February). Wear very warm clothes (thick coat, gloves, hat, waterproof warm boots). Cancellation possible in case of insufficient snow or extreme weather.

The activity is open from age 14 and is a hit with groups of friends, stag/hen parties, teen and adult birthdays, corporate team-building events or couples looking for an original night out. The centre offers coached sessions of about 1h-1h30, lane privatisation and full birthday packages with cake and drinks. The venue also features a full games bar — billiards, foosball, electronic darts, board games — and a selection of local craft beers to extend the evening after your session.
The activity is open from age 14 at XQV0Z. Minors must be accompanied or have parental authorisation per the centre's conditions.

The activity is open to anyone in good health, from around 13-15 years (parental authorisation required for minors), weighing between ~45 and 110 kg depending on the operator. No previous experience is needed: a full briefing is given on site before the jump, equipment checked in front of you, then guided up onto the viaduct by the team. The jump itself lasts a few seconds, but the full experience (briefing, checks, climb, jump, descent, debrief, souvenir photo) takes around 1h30 to 2 hours.

The activity is suited to families with children (from around 8-10 depending on the cave), groups of friends, stag/hen parties, school groups or corporate team-building. No prior experience is needed for discovery outings. Trips run 2 to 4 hours including the approach walk, safety briefing, underground progression (crawling, easy down-climbs, sometimes short rope pitches) and return. For seasoned cavers, the bureau also offers introductions to vertical caving on rope.
The activity is suspended in strong wind, storms or rain. If closed, tickets are refunded or rescheduled. Check the day before with the resort.
The activity is weather-dependent.

The activity runs from mid-December to mid-March depending on snow. January and February are the most reliable months for fresh snow. In December and March, availability depends on the snow cover. Conditions are confirmed the day before.

The actual summit is a ten-minute walk north of this platform, on a limestone ridge that is exposed but secured by cables and barriers at exposed passages. From there, the view spans 180°: Lake XQV0Z below, Semnoz to the southwest, summits of the Bauges massif (Tournette, Dents de Lanfon, Arcalod) to the southeast, the Aravis range and Mont Blanc to the east (visible via Col des Aravis), and the Jura to the north. The ridge continues towards Mont Veyrier for those wishing to extend the hike.
The adjacent

The admission price for the discovery course at XQV0Z is €14 for an adult and €13 for a child. The main course costs €20 for an adult and €18 for a child. Group rate (from 8 people): -€2 per entry. Verified on 14 May 2026.

The adult admission price (15+) for the guided tour of the XQV0Z is €12. Child (5–14): €6. Free for children under 5. Gardens and terraces only ticket: €3. Group rate (10+ people): €10 per person. Verified 14 May 2026.

The adult entrance fee for XQV0Z (from 16 years) is €9. Child (4–15 years): €6. Free under 4 years. Reduced rate: groups (15+ people on reservation), persons with disabilities, hikers who walked or cycled from the valley. 15% of ticket price funds 2027 operations (closure for rehabilitation). Verified 14 May 2026.

The adult price for the XQV0Z is €15. Children 6–18 years: €8.50. Free for children under 6. Family Pass (2 adults + 2 children): €38.50. Student (under 26): €11. Season pass (unlimited entry April–October): €25. Verified 6 June 2026.
The adult rate for XQV0Z is €7. Child 6–16 years: €3.50. Adult group (min. 20 persons): €5.60. Free for under 6 and with Multi Pass Portes du Soleil or Pass XQV1Z France. Verified May 14, 2026.

The adult rate for the Advanced Course at XQV0Z is €27. Junior Course €22. Children's Course €17. Acro'Ninja, Hop Up Challenge and Acro'Filet €17 each. Sensory Forest €5 (free under 3). Treasure Hunt €10. Orienteering €6. Multi-activity pass available. Group rates on request. Verified 5 June 2026.
The adult rate for the main course at XQV0Z is €26. Youth under 16: €20. Children's course: €12 (1 round) or €17 (2 rounds). Group rates from 9 people: €22/adult, €17/youth. Verified May 14, 2026.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z has 8 stations with educational panels, each covering an aspect of alpine-pasture life: cheese, herd, wildlife, flora.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is a family walk of 6.5 km and 2 h 30 — accessible to regular walkers and to children from age 7–8.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is about 6.5 km as a loop, with 330 m of climb, i.e. 2 h 30 to 3 h of walking at a relaxed pace.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is freely accessible from May to October depending on snow cover. Mid-mountain trail — avoid in snow or thunderstorms. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z offers panoramas over the Vallée Verte, a pastoral valley typical of the Pre-Alps of Lake Geneva.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z starts behind the church, letting you discover the village and its heritage before or after the walk.
The alpine-pasture trail is a 6.5 km discovery loop at XQV0Z (Vallée Verte), inspired by Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance'. 8 stations with panels and QR codes on alpine-pasture life.
The alpine-pasture trail itself is entirely free. Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance' (sold at the town hall and TOs) is extra. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The alpine-pasture trail pays tribute to the book 'Alpage de mon enfance' (1977) by Guy Chatiliez, who recalls his childhood memories of the Vallée Verte alpine pastures.
The alpine-pasture trail starts behind the church at XQV0Z (74420). A 10-minute walk from the village centre along a gently descending lane.
The animals are in semi-freedom: it's important to respect the enclosures, not shout, and not feed the animals (feeding strictly controlled by ONF).
The annual fee and playing rates are set by the association and revised each season. For exact 2026 prices, contact the club directly.
The architecture is typical of the late 13th century: a
The area is home to ibex, marmots, and chamois. Early morning offers the best observation chances. Maintain distance from wild animals.

The beach combines a shaded grassy area with a sandy swimming zone supervised in July and August by lifeguards. An amphibious bathing chair (tiralo) is available from the lifeguards to facilitate access for people with reduced mobility. The beach has a dedicated swimming area for dogs (right side facing the lake), one of the rare Lake Geneva beaches to allow this.

The beach comprises a grass area shaded by century-old chestnut trees, popular for picnics and relaxation, and lake access with a pebble and gravel bottom. Swimming is supervised from July 1 to August 31, 10:30 am to 7 pm, with a rescue post and lifeguards on duty. In summer, several thousand walkers and swimmers visit this very family-friendly site daily.

The beach has a wild, preserved feel and is one of the quietest spots in the Bas-Chablais, far less busy than nearby Anthy, Corzent or XQV0Z. The restaurants of Port de Séchex are within walking distance, as is the Bowling de XQV1Z in the village. Bathing is free and not supervised.
The beach has been

The beach has two swimming zones: a shallow paddling pool for children with games, and a deeper zone open to the lake. The bottom is made of small pebbles. The concrete installations facilitate water access and evoke natural swimming pools by the seaside. The beach is equipped with fixed and floating jetties, diving boards, several modern sanitary facilities with showers and changing rooms, as well as a large snack bar with indoor and outdoor seating offering fine views over the lake.
The beach holds the

The beach is accessible year-round and free. The best period for swimming is June to September, with water reaching 20–24°C in July–August. During high season (27 June–31 August), the beach is paid and supervised from 10am to 7pm, with heavy crowds in sunny afternoons — prefer early morning or evening. Off-season (April–May, September–October), access is free, the atmosphere more peaceful, but water cooler (15–18°C). In winter and spring, the beach remains open for lakeside walks, but without supervision or swimming possible.

The beach is accessible year-round, but the ideal bathing season runs from June to September, with water reaching 22 to 25°C in July–August. Supervised swimming operates from 1 July to 30 August. Spring and autumn offer a quieter atmosphere, ideal for walks on the sand and observing dune flora. In winter, the beach becomes a popular birdwatching site, with the calm waters of the Vion stream hosting diverse lakeside wildlife. Summer events (concerts, sand festival, artisan market) are in full swing in July and August. Avoid busy weekends during hot periods in peak season for a more pleasant experience.

The beach is distinguished by its very gentle slope extending several tens of metres, allowing visitors to walk up to a hundred metres with water at waist height. Summer water temperatures reach around 25°C from June to September, making it a preferred bathing location for families. The setting offers panoramic views of Dent d'Oche and the Swiss coast, with pine forest in the background. The beach has held Blue Flag status since 2018, a label guaranteeing water quality and environmental site management.

The beach is equipped with a 3-level diving board (5 metres high) accessible by swimming from pontoons, a paddling pool and play area for young children, a beach-volleyball court and petanque pitch. A restaurant and snack bar are on site. Immediately nearby: XQV0Z marina, the Reed Path trail, and the Lake XQV1Z cycle route (9 km to XQV2Z).
The beach is officially supervised by lifeguards in July and August, with a swimming area marked out by buoys. The local water-sports club next door offers paddle, kayak and catamaran hire, and the
The beach is open year-round but visits vary by season.

The beach is shaded by numerous trees, making it particularly popular with families with young children in summer. It is supervised by lifeguards from the start of summer school holidays until August 31, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Outside the season, the beach remains open for walks, picnics, sports, or simply enjoying the view of the lake and surrounding mountains.

The beach offers a wide sand-and-lawn area with a floating pontoon in summer. The Club Nautique XQV0Z, next door, hires out paddleboards, kayaks and sailing boats. The beach is also the western terminus of the Voie verte du XQV1Z, a great cycling route along the lake.
The beach was awarded the

The best period for XQV0Z extends from June to October. July and August offer the most stable conditions. September and early October are ideal for solitude and light. Late May and early June: persistent snowfields. In winter: ski touring or snowshoeing only.

The best period for XQV0Z is mid-July to late August. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for approximately 9 months per year. In early season (June-early July), snow patches may remain on the trail. Check conditions with La Chamoniarde before departing.

The best period to visit the XQV0Z is April to October, with preference for May, June and September offering pleasant weather and moderate crowds. Summer provides the best conditions for panoramic views over Lake XQV1Z.

The best periods for XQV0Z are spring (April–June) and autumn (September–October). Visitor numbers are calmer than in summer, temperatures are pleasant and the lake view is often clear. Avoid winter (November–March) without suitable equipment: the ridge can be iced.
The best season for XQV0Z is May to October. Summer: strong flow, busy site. Spring/autumn: fewer crowds, soft light. Winter: access depends on snow cover.

The best season for XQV0Z is spring (April–May) for maximal flow with snowmelt, or summer (June–September) for cool forest cover and canyoning. In winter, the cascade can be frozen, spectacular but access is slippery.

The best starting point to discover the Thiou is the Pont des Amours, at the outflow of Lake XQV0Z on the Pâquier side: from there you can head up the canal du Vassé and the Port arm through the old town.
The best time is mid-July to mid-August, when the park is open continuously every day and conditions are optimal. The shaded forest setting provides ideal comfort even on hot days.
The best time to see the waterfalls at XQV0Z is mid-May to end June, during snowmelt: over 30 waterfalls then cascade down the cliffs with spectacular flow.

The best time to swim at XQV0Z is June to September, with 25°C water and supervised swimming in July–August. Spring and autumn are ideal for enjoying calm surroundings and observing the unique dune flora.
The birdwatching observatory at Grande Corne meadow allows observation of nesting and migratory birds (tufted ducks, herons, egrets, sandpipers) while ensuring their tranquility.
The board-game bar and quiz room are accessible. The escape rooms include a few steps — call +33 4 50 05 49 63 to check your specific case.
The book 'Alpage de mon enfance' by Guy Chatiliez is on sale at the XQV0Z town hall and the XQV1Z and XQV2Z Tourist Offices — useful to prepare or extend the alpine-pasture trail.

The booklet for the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is available free of charge at the Chamonix Valley Tourist Offices: XQV1Z, Chamonix, Argentière, XQV2Z, XQV3Z. It can also be downloaded on chamonix.com.

The booklet for the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is available free of charge at the Chamonix Valley Tourist Offices: XQV1Z, Chamonix, XQV2Z, Argentière, XQV3Z. It can also be downloaded on chamonix.com.

The bouldering wall covers all profiles — slabs, vertical, overhangs — with a hold variety suited to every level, from beginners discovering the sport to advanced climbers working on movement and reading. The

The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is a stone dated 1579, set at the foot of the steps near the church of XQV0Z. It is today the only tangible remnant of the seigneurial Château du Vuache, which dominated the village in the Middle Ages.
The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is accessible 24/7, year-round, with free access. Daytime is preferable to read the 1579 date clearly. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is in the heart of the village of XQV0Z (74520), at the foot of the steps immediately next to the church. GPS: 46.101° N · 5.927° E.
The building stands on the Boissy hillside at
The building, sober and harmonious in its classical Grand Siècle style, is today a cultural hub for the Chablais. It houses both the
The cable car is closed from 1 November to 31 March. The trails remain open on foot or with snowshoes depending on snow cover — beware of ice on the rocks.

The cascade is accessible by hiking trail in approximately 30 minutes from the Burzier parking area. It can also be approached through a Via Corda organized by the XQV0Z Guides Bureau (a paid and supervised activity, distinct from free access to the natural site). The cascade cliff is also a renowned climbing site in XQV1Z, with routes ranging from 5c to 8c on gray and orange limestone, facing east.

The cascade is accessible in 5 to 10 minutes' walk from the Parc Biolley parking area in Chedde, via a maintained trail leading to an observation platform. A second unmarked trail allows visitors to approach even closer to the waterfall. The flow is particularly spectacular after heavy rain or in spring during snowmelt.

The castle had an eventful history: converted into the
The castle is now home to the offices of the
The castle now houses the

The castle of XQV0Z was built in the 15th century by the marquises of XQV1Z in Gothic style, enlarged until the 17th century then acquired by the Gerbais de Sonnaz family in 1696. It was burnt down by the SS on the night of 25 to 26 December 1943, during the Christmas 1943 massacre that cost the lives of 32 people.
The castle overlooks the Thiou and offers a complementary reading of XQV0Z's heritage.
The castle preserves architectural elements from the Middle Ages (keep, towers from the 12th and 13th centuries), the Baroque period, and the 19th century. It was the residence of
The castle was
The castle was built by the lords of Langin as a fortified hunting residence, during the expansion phase that peaked in the 15th–16th centuries. In
The castle, built in the early
The cave displays a rich set of calcite formations — stalactites, stalagmites, draperies, rimstones — typical of Alpine karst. Reading the underground landscape with the guide.

The cave has been open to the public since 1906, making it the village's first tourist attraction. It represents the ancient underground course of the torrent and collects water that infiltrates the karst network in the Bauges mountains. The galleries display the circular profile characteristic of pressure conduits, illustrating the geological concept of the "keyhole cave". The guided tour lasts approximately 40 minutes at a constant temperature of 10°C, allowing visitors to discover the spectacular effects of erosion on limestone.
The cave is unsited. Independent visiting is only realistic for self-sufficient confirmed cavers, properly equipped and accompanied. Flood risk must be monitored.

The centre covers all watersports: sailing (Optimist, Laser, catamaran), rowing, kayak and stand-up paddle (SUP). Children's, teenage and adult courses run through the summer season, and equipment is also available to rent. The Marquisats beach is open to the public and offers direct access to Lake XQV0Z from the city centre.
The centre has a 25m six-lane sports pool, a fun pool with current river and massage jets, a paddling pool for toddlers and water slides.
The centre is in XQV0Z (74100), right next to XQV1Z, 15 minutes from Geneva via the Mon-Idée or Moillesulaz border crossings, and 45 minutes from XQV2Z on the A41.
The centre is open to children

The centre is open year-round with different time slots depending on day and period (schools, clubs, public). The weekly schedule is published on the XQV0Z town-hall website. An annual maintenance closure usually takes place in summer or autumn.

The centre is open year-round, with extended hours during school holidays. Public prices are around €5-8 for adults and €3-5 for children; 10-entry cards, monthly/annual passes and reduced rates are available. Swim caps compulsory, soapy shower before swimming. Free parking, bus access from Évian and Thonon. Perfect for an indoor activity alongside a day by the lake.
The centre is served by CCPEVA bus lines from Évian and Thonon. The Lake Geneva cycle greenway runs nearby — ideal by bike from Évian-Amphion.
The centre lets
The centre offers aquabike, aquagym, aquafitness, baby swimming, aquatic garden, swimming lessons for children and adults, and aquaphobia sessions. Swimming schools 1st semester 25/26.

The centre offers three complementary water zones: a children's area (heated paddling pool at 32 °C, water tree, slides, suspended water games), a relaxation pool (gentle lazy river bordered by tropical trees at 30 °C, 1.5 m swimming pool, aquatic climbing wall), and a thrills zone (Slidewinder, a 10 m high tube slide half-pipe style). An outdoor hot tub heated to 33 °C offers breathtaking views of the Portes du Soleil peaks.

The centre suits beginners (group intro lessons, racquet rental) as well as experienced players (free booking, tournaments, intermediate/advanced levels). Private lessons with a coach, children's/teen camps during school holidays, corporate events and birthdays are also available. Padel is one of the fastest-growing sports, accessible and fun, ideal for friend groups.

The centre targets the French-Swiss border area: families (usually from age 8), groups of friends, stag/hen parties, business seminars and schools outside school hours. Packages range from a single game to bundles of 2 or 3 consecutive games at reduced per-game rates. Children's birthday parties are a specialty: private room subject to availability, cake and drinks possible, personalised invitations and souvenir photo. The experience is intense but contactless (IR technology) — closed shoes and comfortable clothing recommended.

The centre welcomes serious swimmers (lanes reserved early morning and at certain times), families with children (fun area, paddling pool, slides), seniors (aquagym, dedicated slots) and school groups. Programme of coached activities: aquagym, aquabike, swimming school, parent-and-baby classes, water garden. The wellness area (sauna, hammam, sensory showers) is available with an extra fee and open to over-16s. Lifeguards are present at all times around the pools.
The centrepiece is a
The château is private property, not open to the public, but is easily visible from the surrounding paths around XQV0Z. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1944.
The château is privately owned and not open to the public, but is clearly visible from the road through XQV0Z.
The château preserves several remarkable decorative features:

The cinema is located in XQV0Z (74700), at the foot of the XQV1Z massif and very close to the resorts of XQV2Z, Cordon, Saint-Gervais and XQV3Z. Tickets are available on site and online (official site or partner platforms such as AlloCiné) for quick booking. Parking is usually available nearby and screens are wheelchair accessible. Concession stand on site.

The cinema joins national operations (Fête du Cinéma in late June/early July, Printemps du Cinéma in March, Rentrée du Cinéma in September) and hosts occasional previews, talks with directors and pop-up cine-clubs. Programming changes every Wednesday — see the official site.

The cinema joins national operations (Fête du Cinéma in late June/early July, Printemps du Cinéma in March, Rentrée du Cinéma in September) and hosts occasional previews, talks with directors and relays. Programming changes every Wednesday — see the official site.

The cinema takes part in national operations: Fête du Cinéma (June/July), Printemps du Cinéma (March), Rentrée du Cinéma (September). Previews, talkback screenings, family sessions and occasional film clubs throughout the year.

The cinema takes part in national operations: Fête du Cinéma (June/July), Printemps du Cinéma (March), Rentrée du Cinéma (September). Previews, talks with directors, family screenings, opera/concert relays.

The cirque is spectacular year-round, with very different atmospheres depending on the season. May–June is the peak period: over 30 waterfalls fed by snowmelt cascade down the cliffs with impressive flow, set among colourful vegetation. July–August: high visitor numbers, heat, fewer waterfalls (about a dozen persist). September–October: low crowds, autumn colours, snow-free peaks, ideal for wildlife watching. December–March: cross-country domain and snowshoeing, frozen waterfalls, monochrome landscape. Check the webcam before you go to verify conditions (haut-giffre.fr).

The cirque's rocks are Jurassic limestone, heavily fractured. High rainfall intensifies erosion: a major rockfall occurred in 1602 (burying several hamlets, commemorated by a chapel), then in 2003 (300,000 m³) and September 2025 (35,000 m³ at Pas Noir), leading to temporary closure of the Fond de la Combe sector by municipal order.

The classic route departs from Bois Brûlé parking (Villaz) via the Grand Montoir (1 h 45, equipped with cables) and descends via the Petit Montoir (easier). For a short access, depart from L'Anglettaz chalet (Aviernoz): 1 h 15 ascent, 382 m elevation gain.
The classic start is at

The climb to the XQV0Z from Morsulaz is short but steep (significant average gradient). The route remains accessible to families used to mountain walking. The gentler Bété trail is currently closed by municipal order.
The club also offers
The club has French billiards (carom) and American (pool) tables. Depending on the slots, other disciplines like snooker may also be played.
The club has a
The club is an association: access is mostly reserved for members. Discovery slots or occasional rentals may be offered depending on availability. Contact the club before travelling.

The club is open to adults and teenagers wishing to practise billiards regularly, as well as occasional players interested in a discovery or hourly table rental (limited slots, subject to club availability). Several tables allow small groups to play in parallel. Coaches and reference members run discovery sessions in French billiards for newcomers, and the team also supervises in-house tournaments and regional inter-club meetings.
The club mainly opens in the evening (weekdays and weekends) for training and matches. Exact hours vary by sports season and are available on the club's site.
The col is also a

The col is generally open from early May to mid-November. In 2026, it opened on 6 May at 2 pm and will close around 18 November depending on snow conditions. Check inforoute74 for the current road state.
The collection holds more than
